author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: How blessed and wonderful, beloved, are the gifts of God!

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Life in immortality, splendour in righteousness, truth in perfect confidence, faith in assurance, self-control in holiness!

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: And all these fall under the cognizance of our understandings [now]; what then shall those things be which are prepared for such as wait for Him?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The Creator and Father of all worlds, the Most Holy, alone knows their amount and their beauty.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let us therefore earnestly strive to be found in the number of those that wait for Him, in order that we may share in His promised gifts.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: But how, beloved, shall this be done?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: If our understanding be fixed by faith towards God; if we earnestly seek the things which are pleasing and acceptable to Him; if we do the things which are in harmony with His blameless will; and if we follow the way of truth, casting away from us all unrighteousness and iniquity, along with all covetousness, strife, evil practices, deceit, whispering, and evil-speaking, all hatred of God, pride and haughtiness, vain glory and ambition.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For they that do such things are hateful to God; and not only they that do them, but also those that take pleasure in them that do them.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For the Scripture saith, “But to the sinner God said, Wherefore dost thou declare my statutes, and take my covenant into thy mouth, seeing thou hatest instruction, and castest my words behind thee?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: When thou sawest a thief, thou consentedst with him, and didst make thy portion with adulterers.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Thy mouth has abounded with wickedness, and thy tongue contrived deceit.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Thou sittest, and speakest against thy brother; thou slanderest thine own mother’s son.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: These things thou hast done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest, wicked one, that I should be like to thyself.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: But I will reprove thee, and set thyself before thee.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Consider now these things, ye that forget God, lest He tear you in pieces, like a lion, and there be none to deliver.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The sacrifice of praise will glorify me, and a way is there by which I will show him the salvation of God.”
